Anyone who covers tech/hacking in any way and portrays Sabu like this: you are very clearly betraying your lack of knowledge about this world and it's history and I refuse to trust your word or judgement on any other related story

It's cute that Sabu has some prison stories from his 7 months in jail, but Jeremy Hammond has been in federal prison since 2013 directly because of the choices Sabu made at the direction of the FBI. Gross to see him parading these stories around like some badge of honor. https://t.co/q59i42wJNc

"Monsegur... said he hasn’t “watched too much of the show,” but noted: “Obviously the people from behind the show have borrowed ideas from Anonymous and from my story.” I don't remember the storyline where Elliot is caught, snitches, cooperates, and sells out his friends, so...

"Esmail has cited Anonymous — and especially LulzSec — as source material for his Emmy award-winning TV show and its anarchistic hacking network known as “F-society.”" - Esmail was likely inspired by people currently serving time for LulzSec actions, like Jeremy Hammond, not Sabu

"A notorious computer hacker whose exploits helped inspire the TV show “Mr. Robot”" - @nypost I don't think there is a single time that Sabu ever actually executed any exploits himself, other than aiding others
